steps.title: Spot the emotional contrast
steps.content: The narrator is dreadfully afraid of the approaching wolves, the horses share his fear, but the driver is not in the least disturbed. The word however signals that despite the same danger, the driver has a completely different emotional state.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Alarmed (B) is correct because it expresses the emotional response to danger that the driver lacks—while the narrator and horses are frightened, the driver shows no alarm.

Disorganized, offended, and interrupted fail to capture this emotional dimension.

B is correct.
